Patron Tiers

Become part of the story.

Octopolis is built by people who believe art can become habitat. Choose a level of patronage — each one carries her further, from the build floor to the playa to her life as a reef. Every tier holds everything below it.

🪸

Coral

$500+

  • Behind-the-scenes build updates from the crew — steel frame to ocean floor
  • A ticket to the Cephalopod Auction
🖋

Ink

$1,000+

  • Everything in Coral
  • A Patron Gift waiting for you on the playa — you'll know what to do with it when the time comes
🐙

Cephalopod

$5,000+

  • Everything in Ink, with a second Patron Gift — one to keep, one to share
  • A shout-out across our media channels — your name travels with Octopolis
⭐

Guardian

$10,000+

  • Everything in Cephalopod
  • A personal welcome and guided tour of Octopolis on the playa with the crew
  • Your name engraved on the Patron Plaque — part of her, permanently
🌊

Sucker

$20,000+

  • Everything in Guardian
  • A personal message engraved into the sculpture herself
  • An underwater guided tour of Octopolis once she settles into her reef home

To claim patron tier perks, send us a message and we'll coordinate your donation personally. For a tax-deductible donation of $500+ via our fiscal sponsor, Creative Reuse Marketplace, please fill out this form.

Events

Come meet us.

July 18, 2026 · Berkeley, CA
How to Become an Octopus
An evening with the Octopolis team and artist Miriam Simun of the Institute for Transhumanist Cephalopod Evolution. Miriam guides a two-hour workshop she created — ‘How To Become An Octopus (and sometimes squid)’ — followed by time together with the crew. 7pm–12am.
Get tickets →
April 24, 2026 · 7–9pm
SF Coming Out Party
Our first public fundraising event. Come meet the team, see the vision, and be part of bringing Octopolis to life.
RSVP →
May 2026
Online Silent Auction
Bid on original artwork and pieces of Octopolis herself. All proceeds go directly to the build.
Bid →

Win a piece of art or a piece of Octopolis herself. Multiple auctions held through 2026.

The Journey

The journey to build a 20-ft. octopus.

From salvaged metal and donated fabric to a living reef — Octopolis travels an extraordinary path from the desert to the ocean floor.

01

The Journey Begins with Salvage

Octopolis begins with a big vision: a 20-foot octopus built from salvaged, found, and donated materials — reclaimed metal and fabric given new purpose.

02

Building the Structure in California

In Northern California, fabricators construct the giant steel framework that forms the body, head, and arms.

03

Building the Skin in Illinois

In Central Illinois, volunteers and artists create the textured skin from hundreds of patchwork fabric pieces sewn into massive panels.

04

Two Parts, One Purpose

Skin and structure are built over 2,000 miles apart, then brought together into a single monumental work of art.

05

Assembly at Burning Man

In August, Octopolis is fully assembled in the Nevada desert, rising as an immersive, larger-than-life experience.

06

Education Through Wonder

More than 50,000 people are expected to explore her interior, learning about octopus intelligence, reefs, and ocean conservation.

07

A Second Life

After the burn she travels to Santa Fe Railyard Park, continuing as a public art installation and educational experience.

08

Beyond Land

Her patchwork skin — carrying the fingerprints of thousands of makers — is removed and repurposed into future art.

09

From Art to Habitat

The structure is deployed into the ocean as part of a coral reef restoration effort, becoming habitat for marine life.

10

A Mother’s Mission

At heart, she tells the story of a mother octopus bringing life back to a dying reef — a symbol of resilience and renewal.

11

A Lasting Legacy

Discarded materials become public art, education, and a living reef: proof of what creativity, collaboration, and heart can build.

Scroll the timeline →

What is Octopolis

She’s two things at once.

Octopolis is a 20-foot illuminated octopus with interactive arms stretching 30–36 feet — a large-scale metal sculpture with integrated lighting, sensors, and multi-sensory elements inside and outside the form.

Her head is a communal gathering space: ocean sounds, bubble effects, cooling misters, and an overhead view of her three glowing hearts. Her eyes are built from locally sourced driftwood.

From sketch to the playa

We build in public.

From the first sketches to the latest dust-and-lights progress — the whole build, out in the open.

Where the Money Goes

$200,000

builds her — and gives her a second life.

Structure & materials, interactive tech, artist fees, transport, on-playa camp for 45 people, post-burn touring, and ocean deployment as a coral restoration site.

Help close the gap →

Join the Crew

45 strong and growing.

Octopolis is built by dreamers, makers, welders, painters, engineers, musicians, and everyone in between. Whether you can swing a hammer or spin a story, there’s a place for you.

We need hands for the build, minds for the plan, and hearts for the mission. Located across the US and beyond, our crew works both on-site and remotely.

Ready to jump in? Tell us your skills and ideas in the form at the top of the page — it takes about a minute and goes straight to the crew.

We’re building in public.

Sketches, sparks, tentacles taking shape. Follow along as Octopolis comes to life.

The Visionary

Stewarded by LaynaJoy.

LaynaJoy is a multifaceted artist and visionary whose work transforms spaces into immersive worlds. She’s the creator of Ravens Landing — an off-grid sustainable sanctuary where art, nature, and community converge — and the driving force behind Octopolis.

She builds large, she builds with intention, and she builds to last.

“Art that begins as a vision and ends as action.”
